title: Lastrea amabilis
taxonomy: Lastrea amabilis is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns native to parts of Asia. The species is further classified as Lastrea amabilis, with scientific names often varying due to ongoing research in the field. More specifically, Lastrea amabilis falls under the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Pteridophyta, and the class Polypodiopsida. Further classification may also include its order (Athyriales), order division (Polypodiales), subclass (Polypodiidae), and division (Pteridophyta).
uses: Lastrea amabilis is a type of fern that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to contribute to its potential health benefits. In traditional medicine, Lastrea amabilis has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of using Lastrea amabilis for medicinal purposes. Its leaves are often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications.
benefits: Lastrea amabilis, also known as the maiden's hair fern, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits.
